</imports>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.54) — 39 imports target "prd-domain-operations"
- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- The 39-import dependency on the core zone confirms this is the primary CLI consumer of domain logic — keep this boundary explicit and avoid leaking domain logic back upward
- The entry-point count (10) is high relative to zone size, suggesting many commands are independently addressable; consider whether a unified command registry would reduce entry-point sprawl
- parent-completion.ts, parent-reset.ts, and timestamps.ts feel like domain helpers that may belong in the core zone rather than the CLI layer — audit their test coverage location to confirm
- Cohesion of 0.46 and coupling of 0.54 are borderline — the zone is holding together but the mix of output formatting, parent lifecycle, and validation utilities suggests it may benefit from a future split into a command-handlers zone and a CLI-support-utilities zone.
- 13 reverse imports from core back into this zone (core → unit-cli) create a bidirectional dependency with the core domain zone; domain code should not import from the CLI layer — identify which files are causing this and relocate them.
- At 37 files this is one of the largest zones; a periodic review to confirm each file is genuinely CLI-layer code (not domain logic that drifted in) will keep cohesion from degrading further.
- Zone "rex-cli-command-layer" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The cli → unit-cli: 13 cross-zone import (MCP interface zone importing from CLI command layer) indicates the MCP server implementation is coupled to CLI command internals rather than domain operations directly — MCP tools should depend on domain logic, not CLI formatting helpers
- unit-cli → unit-3: 11 imports alongside core → unit-3: 20 shows both CLI and domain layers share a tree-primitives dependency; if unit-3 is dissolved into prd-domain-operations, CLI gains an indirect coupling that may deepen the bidirectional cycle rather than resolve it
- cli-mcp-interface imports 13 symbols from rex-cli-command-layer, creating a lateral dependency between two CLI-tier zones that should both depend downward on domain — the MCP interface is reaching into CLI command internals instead of calling domain functions directly
- rex-cli-command-layer is simultaneously imported by three zones (prd-domain-operations 13, cli-mcp-interface 13, integration-adapter-layer 2) making it a secondary hub in the CLI tier; any CLI utility extracted here automatically acquires three upstream dependents
- The single concrete domain→CLI tier inversion has been identified: src/core/move.ts imports CLIError from src/cli/errors.ts. This is the only actual runtime coupling from domain code into the CLI layer; the remaining apparent 'core → unit-cli' imports are artifacts of zone misclassification of add.ts and reorganize.ts
- src/cli/errors.ts itself imports REX_DIR from src/cli/commands/constants.ts, meaning the transitive chain from core/move.ts is: domain → CLI error class → CLI command constants — three tiers of CLI infrastructure pulled into the domain layer through a single import
- CLIError is semantically a shared package error type (used by both domain and CLI layers) but is physically located in the CLI layer — relocating it to src/errors.ts at the package root would break the entire concrete domain→CLI cycle with a single file move and no logic changes
- CLIError is defined in src/cli/errors.ts but imported by src/core/move.ts (a domain file), creating a tier inversion. The class belongs in a package-level src/errors.ts that both core/ and cli/ can import without cycle risk
- src/cli/errors.ts imports REX_DIR from src/cli/commands/constants.ts, creating a transitive dependency chain core/move.ts → cli/errors.ts → cli/commands/constants.ts; domain code indirectly depends on CLI command constants
- rex-cli-command-layer is missing two major commands (next.ts and fix.ts) that live in satellite micro-zones — a developer auditing the CLI command surface from this zone sees an incomplete picture; the zone should either absorb these commands or link to them explicitly in zone documentation
- The zone spans 37 files but excludes 2 CLI commands that are structurally identical to its members — this selective inclusion is invisible to contributors and will cause future CLI commands to be placed inconsistently depending on which zone is used as a reference
- Pin next.ts and fix.ts (currently in satellite micro-zones) to rex-cli-command-layer to create a complete, auditable CLI command surface in one zone — the satellite micro-zones exist only due to test co-location, not genuine architectural separation
- [call graph] 1091 internal calls, 98 outgoing, 413 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
